texto: Don't creationists ever wonder about the fact that the paleontologists found ape-like skulls with the 'human leg and foot bones,' rather than the other way around, i.e., human skulls with 'ape leg and foot bones?' . . . Come on, creationists, think about it! Did God hide the human skulls, only leaving behind leg and foot bones belonging to human midgets with misshapen feet, and mix such bones only with the skulls of ape-like creatures with larger cranial capacities than living apes? What a 'kidder' the creationists' God must be.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Edward T. Babinski: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Don't creationists ever wonder about the fact that the paleontologists found ape-like skulls with…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, atheism, atheist pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de atheist e atheists.
